I'll start by reading the main file and understanding what changes need to be made.

Now let me analyze each fix:

**Fix 1 (Line 76):** `Button.variant` type changed — but the value used is `ButtonVariant.link`, and `'link'` is still a valid value in the new type union. This is a no-op; no change needed.

**Fix 2 (Line 17):** `Icon` component from `@patternfly/react-core` was removed. The `EmptyStateIconProps` and `IconProps` interfaces were removed. Looking at the code, `Icon` is used on line 84 as a wrapper around `ArrowRightIcon`. In PF6, the standalone `Icon` component was removed. The icon can be used directly without the `Icon` wrapper.

Let me check how `Icon` is used and what the proper PF6 replacement is:

Now I have a clear picture. Let me reason through each fix:

**Fix 1 (Line 76):** `ButtonVariant.link` — the value `'link'` is present in BOTH the old and new type unions. The only change is the addition of `'stateful'`. The current code using `ButtonVariant.link` is already valid. **No change needed.**

**Fix 2 (Line 17):** The `Icon` component is imported from `@patternfly/react-core` and used on line 84-86 to wrap `ArrowRightIcon`. In PF6, the standalone `Icon` component was removed (along with `EmptyStateIconProps` and `IconProps`). The fix is to:
1. Remove the `Icon` import from line 17
2. Replace the `<Icon size="sm">` wrapper with just the icon directly. The `size="sm"` was an `Icon` wrapper prop — for the icon itself, PatternFly icons accept a `style` or we can just use the icon directly since it's inline within a Button.

Looking at the usage:
```jsx
<Icon size="sm">
  <ArrowRightIcon className="pf-v6-u-ml-sm" />
</Icon>
```

In PF6, without the `Icon` wrapper, the `ArrowRightIcon` can be used directly. The `size="sm"` was controlling icon sizing — PF icons accept a `size` prop directly in PF6 (via `@patternfly/react-icons`), but since this is inside a `link` variant Button with `isInline`, the icon will inherit appropriate sizing. I'll just remove the `Icon` wrapper and keep the `ArrowRightIcon` directly.

Let me check the test file — my changes remove the `Icon` wrapper, which shouldn't affect any test assertions since the tests only check for text content and button roles, not the icon structure.

Now let me apply the changes:

**Applied.** Two edits made:
1. **Removed `Icon` import** from the `@patternfly/react-core` import block (line 17).
2. **Removed `<Icon size="sm">` wrapper** around `<ArrowRightIcon>` (lines 84–87 → line 83). The `ArrowRightIcon` is now rendered directly as a child of `Button`, which is the correct PF6 pattern since the standalone `Icon` component was removed.

**No changes needed.** The tests only check text content (`getByText`) and button roles (`getByRole`), none of which are affected by removing the `Icon` wrapper. No portal rendering, OUIA selectors, or attribute-based assertions are involved.

Apply ALL of the following 2 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 33
Rule [family:Modal]:
## Modal Family Migration

Target structure (correct v6 composition):
```jsx
  <Modal appendTo={...} backdropClassName={...} disableFocusTrap={...} elementToFocus={...} id={...} isOpen={...} maxWidth={...} onClose={...}>
    <ModalBody role={...} />
    <ModalHeader description={...} descriptorId={...} help={...} labelId={...} title={...} titleIconVariant={...} titleScreenReaderText={...} />
    <ModalFooter />
  </Modal>
```

Props that stay on <Modal>: appendTo, backdropClassName, disableFocusTrap, elementToFocus, id, isOpen, maxWidth, onClose, onEscapePress, ouiaId, ouiaSafe, position, positionOffset, variant, width

Props that move to child components:
  description -> <ModalHeader />
  help -> <ModalHeader />
  title -> <ModalHeader />
  titleIconVariant -> <ModalHeader />

Removed props (move to child component as children or remove):
  actions -> map to appropriate child component (any)
  bodyAriaLabel -> ModalBody (as children, string)
  bodyAriaRole -> ModalBody (as children, string)
  footer -> ModalFooter (as children, ReactNode)
  hasNoBodyWrapper -> map to appropriate child component (boolean)
  header -> ModalHeader (as children, ReactNode)
  showClose -> map to appropriate child component (boolean)
  titleLabel -> map to appropriate child component (string)

Removed children (no longer valid JSX): ModalBoxCloseButton, ModalContent, ModalBox, ModalBoxBody, ModalBoxHeader, ModalBoxFooter

Add imports: ModalBody, ModalHeader, ModalFooter from '@patternfly/react-core'

Remove imports (if no longer used): ModalBoxCloseButton, ModalContent, ModalBox, ModalBoxBody, ModalBoxHeader, ModalBoxFooter

Deprecated -> v6 migration:
  Old import: @patternfly/react-core
  New import: @patternfly/react-core
Matching props:
New props on replacement:
  backdropClassName: string
Removed props (no replacement equivalent): actions, bodyAriaLabel, bodyAriaRole, description, footer, hasNoBodyWrapper, header, help, showClose, title, titleIconVariant, titleLabel

Incidents found in this file:
  Line 33: [sd-prop-to-children-modal-header-to-modalheader]
    componentName: Modal
    module: @patternfly/react-core
    propName: header
    propValue: Header
    Fix strategy:
      Strategy: PropToChildren
      Component: Modal
      From: header
      Replacement: ModalHeader

Code contexts:
  (line 33):
   32        variant={ModalVariant.medium}
   33        header={Header}
   34        onClose={closeModal}

```
(no snippet)
```

### Fix 2
Line: 75
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: ModalBoxFooter

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
   74        <ModalFooter>
   75          <Button
   76            variant={ButtonVariant.link}

```

I'll start by reading the main file and the test file.

Now let me analyze both fixes:

### Fix 1 — `header` prop on Modal (Line 32)

The code currently has `header={Header}` on the `<Modal>`. In PF6, the `header` prop was removed. The `Header` variable (line 27) is already a `<ModalHeader>` element. The fix is to move the `<ModalHeader>` from the `header` prop to be a direct child of `<Modal>`.

However, I notice the `ModalHeader` and `ModalBody` and `ModalFooter` are imported from `@odf/shared/modals/Modal` (line 4-8), NOT from `@patternfly/react-core`. This is a custom wrapper. Let me check — the rule says to use PF6's `ModalHeader`, but this code already imports its own `ModalHeader` from a shared module.

Looking at the code, the `Header` variable on line 27 creates `<ModalHeader>{headerText}</ModalHeader>` using the shared import. The `header` prop on `<Modal>` passes this as a ReactNode. In PF6, the `header` prop is removed, and content should be placed as children of `<Modal>`. I should move the `Header` content to be a direct child inside `<Modal>`, and remove the `header` prop.

### Fix 2 — Icon children to `icon` prop on Button (Line 74-84)

The Button on line 74-84 has both text content and an `<ArrowRightIcon>`. In PF6, the icon should be passed via the `icon` prop. Since there's both text AND an icon, I move just the icon to the `icon` prop.

Let me apply both fixes:

Now apply Fix 2 — move the `ArrowRightIcon` from children to the `icon` prop on the Button:

          variant={ButtonVariant.link}
          onClick={() => {

            navigate('/multicloud/applications');

        >

        </Button>

            navigate('/multicloud/applications');

content.text: Edited /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/odf-console/source/packages/mco/components/modals/protected-applications/managed-applications-modal.tsx (11 lines -> 11 lines)
Now let me verify the final state of the file:

The file looks correct. Now let me review the test file to check if any of the changes would cause test failures:

The test file (`managed-applications-modal.spec.tsx`) does:
1. **Line 27**: `screen.getByText('Enroll ACM managed application')` — Still works. The `ModalHeader` is now a direct child of `Modal` instead of passed via `header` prop, but the text is still rendered.
2. **Line 30**: `screen.getByRole('button', { name: /Close/i })` — This queries for the close button. PF6's `<Modal onClose={...}>` still renders a close button. No change needed.
3. **Lines 33-36**: `screen.getByText(...)` for body content — Still works, `ModalBody` is unchanged.
4. **Lines 38-40**: `screen.getByText('Continue to Applications page')` — Still works. The text is still a child of `Button`, just the icon moved to a prop.
5. **Line 47**: `screen.getByText('Continue to Applications page')` click — Still works.

No test changes are needed. The test assertions all query by text content and button role, none of which are affected by moving `header` to children or moving the icon to the `icon` prop.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `header` prop on Modal → child (Line 32→35)
**Applied.** Removed the `header={Header}` prop from `<Modal>` and placed `{Header}` as a direct child inside `<Modal>`, before `<ModalBody>`. This follows PF6's composition model where `ModalHeader` is a child of `Modal` rather than passed via the removed `header` prop. Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
